behavior_summary: U8P_KITH::KITH::look is an event handler invoked when the KITH object is 'looked at' (inspected) by an actor/player.
On entry it configures object info/processing state (executes a 'set info' and a 'process exclude' operation) to adjust context or recipients for the upcoming message.
It constructs a short literal string "kith ", pushes the object's pid and the target process parameter, and calls Item::bark to deliver the text to the target process (likely the viewer).
After the bark call the transient string is freed and the bark return value is consumed (passed through an 'implies' and stored in a temp), then the handler suspends.
notable_control_flow: Entry: Event 0 (U8P_KITH::look) — immediate setup of object info/process-exclude flags, followed by a single text-emission call.
Single external interaction: call to Item::bark to emit the textual description to a target process.
Post-call cleanup: free transient string and process the bark return value via an 'implies' before suspending.
No global persistent state appears to be modified by this script beyond the transient info/process-exclude writes.
open_questions: What exact semantic effect does the 'set info' opcode write have here (which info key and how it changes engine behavior)? (appears inferred; confidence: medium)
What precisely does 'process exclude' exclude — recipients for the bark, AI processing, or something else? (likely; confidence: medium)
Is BP+06h always the viewer/player process id, or can other process identifiers be passed to this handler? (inferred; confidence: medium)
What side-effects, if any, are implied by the bark return value and the subsequent 'implies' operation before suspend? (uncertain; confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_KITH::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a 'U8P_KITH::look' (inspect) event by emitting a short descriptive message ('kith ') to a target process, after setting local info/processing flags and performing cleanup, then suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_KITH::look) — called when an actor/player looks at or inspects the KITH object (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_KITH::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_KITH::look' event: set up context, emit the item's short description via Item::bark, clean up transient data, then suspend.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to deliver/display short text messages from an item/process to a target process/player.

external_calls.purpose: Emit/display the literal string 'kith ' to the target process (the handler pushes the target process param and the source object's pid before calling).

writes.name: info (set info opcode)
writes.likely_effect: Configures a transient info/state key on the object to influence subsequent behavior or message context (inferred).

writes.name: process exclude
writes.likely_effect: Marks/excludes certain processes from normal processing or from receiving subsequent messages; used here to shape the bark's recipients or processing window (inferred).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_KITH::KITH::look is an event handler invoked when the KITH object is 'looked at' (inspected) by an actor/player.
